title: cassinia laevis subsp. rosmarinifolia
intro: Cassinia laevis subsp. rosmarinifolia, also known as a shrub-like plant, is a subspecies of the Cassinia laevis species, native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Cassinia laevis subsp. rosmarinifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
